mnemonics: CUT the CONNECTivity: A Cut set is a set of edges whose removal disconnects the graph. A Cut Vertex is a single vertex whose removal disconnects the graph.
keyPoints: A cut set is a minimal set of edges whose removal increases the number of connected components.
A cut vertex (or articulation point) is a vertex whose removal increases the number of connected components.
Every edge in a tree is a cut set, and every non-leaf vertex in a tree is a cut vertex.
commonMistakes: Assuming any set of edges that disconnects a graph is a cut set (it must be minimal).
Confusing a cut set with a vertex cut (a set of vertices whose removal disconnects the graph).
